title: This Merit Hath The Worst,
content:
This merit hath the worst, --
    It cannot be again.
    When Fate hath taunted last
    And thrown her furthest stone,

    The maimed may pause and breathe,
    And glance securely round.
    The deer invites no longer
    Than it eludes the hound.
meaning:
"This Merit Hath The Worst," is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
